ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked broccoli; no tandoori-specific preparation code exists. Closest parent match used for cooked broccoli.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for tandoori broccoli dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Tandoori Broccoli is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a generous side-dish portion of spiced, tandoor-grilled broccoli florets.

nutrient_source.title: Tandoori Broccoli — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per_100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Tandoori Broccoli (cr_id G00010852), confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on broccoli (GI ~15) and yogurt-marinated spiced vegetable preparation
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tandoori Broccoli. GI estimated at 25 based on: (1) raw broccoli GI of approximately 15 per University of Sydney GI Database; (2) upward adjustment for yogurt marinade and moderate carbohydrate content (22.4 g/100g) which includes coating/marinade contribution; (3) spices (turmeric, cumin, chilli) do not materially raise GI. Estimate is conservative and defensible. GL per 150 g serving = (25 × 22.41 × 1.5) / 100 = 8.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 329 mg/100g is in the borderline-elevated range for a vegetable dish, with limited potassium offset (161 mg/100g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100 g or request reduced-salt marinade preparation to bring sodium below 200 mg/100g; the potassium-to-sodium ratio is unfavourable at this sodium level and should be balanced with additional high-potassium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~25), low sugar (0.88 g/100g), and moderate carbohydrate (22.41 g/100g) with protein (4.28 g/100g) providing glycaemic modulation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tandoori Broccoli is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low glycaemic index and low sugar content; the moderate carbohydrate level (22.4 g/100g) likely reflects the yogurt marinade and should be accounted for in total carbohydrate budgeting, particularly if consumed in large portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat at 12.88 g/100g is moderate; saturated fat breakdown is not reported by HPB but cholesterol is low at 11 mg/100g; sodium at 329 mg/100g is borderline elevated.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tandoori Broccoli may be consumed in moderation by individuals with cardiovascular disease; the broccoli base provides anti-inflammatory sulforaphane and fibre, but the sodium content (329 mg/100g) and uncharacterised fat profile warrant portion control and preference for reduced-oil, reduced-salt preparation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Broccoli sulforaphane and anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, ginger-garlic) provide clinically relevant anti-inflammatory benefit; saturated fat is below the 5 g RA threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tandoori Broccoli is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory ingredient profile; nightshade-sensitive individuals should monitor for joint flare from chilli powder, and preparation should favour grilling over deep-frying to minimise advanced glycation end-product formation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tandoori Broccoli is an Indian-origin dish popular in Singapore's restaurant and hawker-adjacent dining scene, consisting of fresh broccoli florets marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture — typically incorporating turmeric, cumin, coriander, chilli powder, ginger-garlic paste, and salt — then cooked at high heat in a traditional clay tandoor oven. The tandoor imparts a characteristic smoky char and slight crispness to the outer florets while retaining a tender interior. The dish carries the aromatic warmth of Indian spice blends and a mild tang from the yogurt marinade. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 223 kcal, 4.28 g protein, 12.88 g fat, 22.41 g carbohydrate, 0.88 g sugar, 1.7 g dietary fibre, and 329 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 25 (low GI), making it suitable for blood glucose management. The broccoli base contributes sulforaphane, a clinically studied anti-inflammatory compound, and the turmeric provides curcumin with established anti-inflammatory properties. Sodium at 329 mg/100g is borderline elevated and warrants caution for hypertensive individuals. The dish is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and gluten-free when prepared without flour coating. It is commonly served as a starter or side dish at Indian restaurants in Singapore and is appropriate for lunch and dinner occasions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in tandoori broccoli?
Is tandoori broccoli good for diabetics?
Is tandoori broccoli high in sodium?
What spices are used in tandoori broccoli?
Can I eat tandoori broccoli if I have hypertension?
What is the GI of tandoori broccoli?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo Singapore database entry for Tandoori Broccoli (cr_id G00010852) with confidence 0.95. Source is an approved primary regional source. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence medium for GI component only); overall record confidence is high given exact HPB match for macros.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Tandoori Broccoli not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry; no equivalent breakdown available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation as of 2026-04-16. 2. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB source entry for this dish. 3. Input food_group was 'Grains and staples' and food_subgroup was 'Baking ingredients' — both are clearly incorrect for a tandoori vegetable dish; corrected to 'Vegetables' and 'Cooked vegetables — tandoor-prepared' respectively. 4. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate based on broccoli GI (~15, University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted upward for yogurt marinade and moderate carbohydrate content; no direct GI measurement for Tandoori Broccoli exists in approved sources. 5. Carbohydrate at 22.41 g/100g is higher than expected for plain broccoli (~7 g/100g); this likely reflects the yogurt marinade, spice coating, and possible oil contribution — verify preparation method if carbohydrate accuracy is critical.
